preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.22.1: Hotfix — chat image-cards now render correctly on Windows installs where os.homedir() returns a different case than the filesystem reports (e.g. C:UsersRyan vs C:UsersRYAN). Live anchor 2026-05-11: paying user reported every chat image card displayed "Image unavailable" placeholder even though files existed on disk and v1.22.0 already shipped the 0-byte retry guard (2026-05-06) plus the gallery-viewer retry-on-error path (2026-05-04). Root cause: the merlin:// protocol handler used case-sensitive JavaScript String.startsWith for containment checks between filePath (absolute path emitted by the Go binary, preserving filesystem case) and resolvedRoot (built from os.homedir/USERPROFILE, which on Windows can return a different case for the same canonical path). When the cases disagreed, every merlin:// request returned 403 Forbidden — the existing retry-on-error logic could not recover because the 403 was permanent on each attempt. Fix: new pathContains and pathEquals helpers do case-insensitive comparison on win32 only (still byte-exact on macOS/Linux whose filesystems are case-sensitive). MIME-type allowlist and file existence checks still gate access — only the prefix string comparison loosens. Locked with main-merlin-protocol-winpath.test.js (4 source-scan tests). Hard-Won Security Rule preserved: the appRoot containment + extension allowlist + 0-byte rejection chain still bounds renderer-initiated filesystem access; case-folding on win32 only loosens the comparison to match the underlying case-insensitive filesystem semantics.
whatsNew: Fixes "Image unavailable" placeholders in chat on Windows installs with case-mismatched paths
No behavior change on macOS/Linux — those filesystems are case-sensitive and pass through the original byte-exact comparison
Defense-in-depth source-scan test prevents the raw startsWith form from sneaking back in
